mechanism_narrative: Sustained high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes real corporate earnings and consumer purchasing power, leading to a downward re-evaluation of equity valuations and negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show no significant negative correlation with CPIAUCSL year-over-year percentage change over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and rapid increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market, which reduces consumer spending and corporate profits, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a longer horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances where UNRATE rises by more than 0.5 percentage points over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: Robust retail sales (RSAFS) combined with an accommodative monetary policy (low FEDFUNDS) indicates strong consumer demand supported by cheap credit, which boosts corporate revenues and equity prices, leading to positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: The interaction term of RSAFS month-over-month growth and FEDFUNDS level shows no significant positive correlation with SPX 20d returns (Rank IC < 0.03).

mechanism_narrative: During periods of aggressive monetary tightening, rapidly rising FEDFUNDS rates increase the cost of capital for businesses and consumers, compressing valuations and leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are positive in more than 40% of instances during identified monetary tightening cycles, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Robust housing starts (HOUST) signal strong construction activity and consumer confidence, which translates into broader economic growth and positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 5d returns are negative or flat in more than 55% of periods when HOUST is above 1500 (thousands of units).

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in industrial production (INDPRO) signals a contraction in manufacturing and overall supply-side activity, negatively impacting corporate revenues and S&P 500 forward returns.
failure_condition: SPX 20d returns show a positive or neutral response to two consecutive months of INDPRO decline in more than 60% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) indicates a strong and expanding labor market, supporting consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 returns over a medium-term horizon.
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative in more than 35% of periods where PAYEMS shows sustained growth above 0.1% over 3 months.

mechanism_narrative: High GDP growth (GDPC1) combined with moderate inflation (CPIAUCSL) creates an ideal 'Goldilocks' environment for corporate profitability and equity market appreciation, as growth is strong without significant cost pressures.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annual growth > 2% AND CPIAUCSL annual change < 3%
failure_condition: SPX 60d returns are negative or flat in more than 50% of periods where GDPC1 growth is strong and CPIAUCSL inflation is moderate, or the average return is not significantly positive.
